message:
test(fcp-crypto/metamorphic): add MR 6 Ed25519 signature adversarial corruption

Add a new metamorphic relation to the fcp-crypto test suite pinning
the AEAD-style authenticity contract of Ed25519 signatures: any
single-byte mutation of a valid signature's 64 bytes MUST cause
verify() to reject.

Why this matters: ed25519-dalek is a pinned external dependency. If
it's accidentally downgraded, monkey-patched by a conflicting feature
flag, or replaced via a malicious crates.io mirror, the most common
failure mode is "signature verification silently accepts mutated
inputs" — which is exactly what defeats the zone-ownership proofs
elsewhere in the workspace. A targeted metamorphic test that flips
one byte and asserts rejection catches the downgrade class without
needing to construct a full signed-envelope scenario.

## The relation

  input:  (sk_seed, message, byte_index ∈ 0..64, xor_mask ∈ 1..=255)
  steps:
    sk       = Ed25519SigningKey::from_bytes(&sk_seed)
    sig      = sk.sign(&message)
    bytes    = sig.to_bytes().to_vec()
    bytes[byte_index] ^= xor_mask
    mutated  = Ed25519Signature::from_bytes(&bytes)
  contract:
    sk.verifying_key().verify(&message, &mutated) must return Err

The xor_mask is drawn from 1..=255 (never 0) so the byte actually
changes — otherwise the property would be trivially "verify accepts
the original signature" which the other MRs already cover.

## Proptest config

256 cases per run (double the default 128) because the state space is
small (64 positions × 255 masks × arbitrary message bytes), so higher
sample count tightens the rejection bound without increasing runtime
much. All other config defaults apply.

## Imports

Add `Ed25519Signature` to the existing ed25519 import and `HpkeSealedBox`
to hpke_seal (the latter was needed for a separate HPKE MR that shares
this file; consolidating imports to avoid churn on the next MR add).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
